title: The 7-Day Strength Form Reset
excerpt: Refine technique, prevent injury, and build stronger movement patterns in just one week.

sections.title: Why Form Matters
sections.content: Proper exercise technique improves performance, reduces injury risk, and ensures the target muscles are doing the work.

sections.title: Day 1: Squat Mechanics
sections.content: Focus on stance width, bracing, and depth. Practice bodyweight squats before loading.

sections.title: Day 2: Hip Hinge Fundamentals
sections.content: Learn how to move through the hips while maintaining a neutral spine.

sections.title: Day 3: Pressing Technique
sections.content: Improve shoulder positioning and pressing mechanics for safer upper-body training.

sections.title: Day 4: Pulling Mechanics
sections.content: Develop proper rowing and pull-up movement patterns.

sections.title: Day 5: Core Stability
sections.content: Practice bracing and anti-rotation exercises to create a stronger foundation.

sections.title: Day 6: Movement Review
sections.content: Review each movement pattern and identify weaknesses.

sections.title: Day 7: Assessment
sections.content: Retest your movements and compare them to Day 1.

title: Beginner's Guide to Progressive Overload
excerpt: Learn the most important principle for building strength and muscle.

sections.title: What Is Progressive Overload?
sections.content: Progressive overload means gradually increasing training demands over time.

sections.title: Ways to Progress
sections.content: Increase weight, reps, sets, frequency, or improve exercise quality.

sections.title: Common Mistakes
sections.content: Adding weight too quickly and sacrificing form are the most common errors.

title: Nutrition Fundamentals
excerpt: Understand calories, macronutrients, and healthy eating habits.

sections.title: Calories
sections.content: Calories are units of energy. Weight change depends largely on energy balance.

sections.title: Protein
sections.content: Protein supports muscle repair and recovery.

sections.title: Carbohydrates
sections.content: Carbs are the body's preferred energy source during exercise.

sections.title: Fats
sections.content: Dietary fats support hormones and overall health.

title: Protein Intake Explained
excerpt: How much protein do you really need for muscle growth and recovery?

sections.title: Why Protein Matters
sections.content: Protein provides amino acids needed for tissue repair and growth.

sections.title: Daily Targets
sections.content: Active individuals generally benefit from higher protein intake than sedentary individuals.

sections.title: Protein Sources
sections.content: Lean meats, fish, eggs, dairy, legumes, and protein supplements are common options.

title: Mastering the Squat
excerpt: Improve depth, stability, and power with better squat mechanics.

sections.title: Setup
sections.content: Position your feet shoulder-width apart and brace your core.

sections.title: Descent
sections.content: Sit down between your hips while maintaining a neutral spine.

sections.title: Ascent
sections.content: Drive through the mid-foot and stand up powerfully.

title: Deadlift Form Guide
excerpt: Build strength safely by mastering the hip hinge movement.

sections.title: Setup
sections.content: Stand with the bar over the middle of your foot.

sections.title: Brace
sections.content: Take a deep breath and create full-body tension.

sections.title: Lift
sections.content: Push the floor away while maintaining spinal neutrality.

title: Recovery and Sleep
excerpt: Learn why recovery is where progress actually happens.

sections.title: Why Recovery Matters
sections.content: Training creates stress. Recovery allows adaptation.

sections.title: Sleep
sections.content: Sleep quality strongly influences performance and recovery.

sections.title: Recovery Habits
sections.content: Hydration, nutrition, and stress management all contribute to recovery.

title: Building Your First Workout Plan
excerpt: Create a sustainable training program that matches your goals.

sections.title: Choose Your Goal
sections.content: Define whether your primary goal is strength, muscle gain, fat loss, or general fitness.

sections.title: Select Exercises
sections.content: Prioritize compound movements before isolation exercises.

sections.title: Plan Progression
sections.content: Track performance and gradually increase training demands.
